dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6142 IH: Ask the Union Members Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Harris of North Carolina (for himself, Mr. Onder, and Mr. Fine) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To require union contract votes to be carried out through a secret ballot election, to prohibit unions from authorizing strikes unless a majority of members of the union vote to authorize a strike, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Ask the Union Members Act.

section.header: Secret ballot for ratification and strike authorization

subsection.text: Section 101(a) of the Labor-Management Reporting and Disclosure Act of 1959 (29 U.S.C. 411(a)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: A collective bargaining agreement may not be executed by a labor organization unless such agreement is approved to be ratified by a majority vote of the members of such labor organization in good standing voting in a referendum conducted by secret ballot, after providing such agreement to each member of the labor organization not later than 72 hours prior to such referendum.

subsection.text: Section 8(b) of the National Labor Relations Act (29 U.S.C. 158(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (6), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (7), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:
to authorize a strike, unless such strike is approved to be authorized by a majority vote of the members in good standing voting in a referendum conducted by secret ballot.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this Act shall take effect beginning on the date that is 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act.
